research_basis: W3C WCAG 2.2 is a W3C Recommendation and adds new success criteria for focus, dragging, target size, consistent help, redundant entry, and accessible authentication.
WCAG requires keyboard operability for interface functionality.
rules: At least one operator profile is required.
At least one context constraint is required.
WCAG 2.2 core criteria must include keyboard, focus not obscured, dragging alternatives, target size, consistent help, and redundant entry.
At least one accessibility requirement must be blocking.
At least one platform requirement is required.
UX risks must include detection and mitigation.
